sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: IIT Madras & Texas Instruments India invite applications for MS Programme
feed.summary: IIT Madras and Texas Instruments India are inviting applications for their joint MS program in Analog, Mixed-Signal (AMS), and RF VLSI Design. This three-year blended program combines a year of classroom learning at IIT Madras with a two-year internship at Texas Instruments, offering comprehensive industry exposure and full scholarship. It aims to develop expertise in niche areas like AMS and RF design, which are in high demand globally.

feed.title: Taiwan chip giant plans big investment as rivalry intensifies
feed.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co (TSMC) announced plans to invest US$100 billion over the next three years to significantly expand its chip fabrication capacity. This massive investment comes as the company faces booming demand for advanced semiconductors and aims to maintain its leading position in the industry amid a global chip supply crunch. Other major players like Intel and Samsung are also heavily investing in semiconductor foundries to meet increasing demand.

feed.title: Salesforce paid no federal income tax in 2020 despite $2.6 billion in profit
feed.summary: Salesforce, despite recording $2.6 billion in net income in its 2021 fiscal year, paid no federal income tax in 2020 and is receiving a $12 million refund. This was revealed in a report by the Institute for Tax and Economic Policy, noting that the company utilized benefits such as writing off executive stock-option-related expenses. Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff had previously supported San Francisco's Proposition C, which increased the company's city taxes, while the company took no position on federal tax cuts.

feed.title: AMD, Salesforce, HP, Xilinx and Sanmina paid $0 in federal income taxes in 2020 despite $4.1B in combined profits
feed.summary: Five Bay Area tech firms, including AMD and Salesforce, reportedly paid no federal income taxes in 2020 despite combined profits of $4.1 billion. The companies legally achieved this by utilizing various credits and maneuvers. AMD, led by CEO Lisa Su, has not paid federal income taxes for the last three years, despite earning $1.7 billion during that period.

feed.title: Intel Launches Its Most Advanced Performance Data Center Platform
feed.summary: Intel has launched its 3rd Gen Intel Xeon Scalable processors (code-named “Ice Lake”), marking its most advanced performance data center platform. These processors offer an average 46% increase in performance for popular data center workloads, alongside built-in AI acceleration and advanced security features. The platform is optimized for a broad range of applications across cloud, network, and intelligent edge environments, with significant industry adoption already underway.

feed.title: Intel Sets Ice Lake Apart From AMD EPYC With AI, Systems Approach
feed.summary: Intel has launched its third-generation Xeon Scalable CPUs, codenamed Ice Lake, emphasizing AI and cryptography acceleration and a comprehensive systems approach to compete with AMD's EPYC processors. Despite AMD's higher core counts, Intel highlights its processors' performance gains in specific AI, cloud, and HPC workloads due to specialized accelerators and software optimizations. Intel's strategy also includes a broader product portfolio and continuous software enhancements to improve overall data center performance and efficiency.

feed.title: Gelsinger Leads Emboldened Intel With Ice Lake Launch
feed.summary: Intel's new CEO, Pat Gelsinger, has launched the 3rd Gen Xeon Scalable processors, "Ice Lake," aimed at reasserting the company's dominance in the server chip market. These new chips feature integrated AI acceleration and security capabilities, backed by strong support from OEMs and public cloud providers. Gelsinger also outlined his "IDM 2.0" strategy, including a $20 billion plan to expand manufacturing fabs and the re-establishment of a major annual event, Intel On, to showcase the company's capabilities.

feed.title: The Alienware m15 Ryzen Edition R5 is an AMD-NVIDIA combo over a decade in the making
feed.summary: The Alienware m15 Ryzen Edition R5 is announced, marking the first Alienware notebook in over a decade to combine AMD processors with NVIDIA graphics. This gaming laptop features Ryzen 5000 series CPUs and NVIDIA RTX 30-series GPUs, along with other advancements like 3200Mhz DDR4 memory and a CHERRY-based mechanical keyboard. It will be available starting April 7th in China, April 20th in the US, and May 4th globally, with select configurations beginning at $1793.98.

feed.title: Intel’s 10nm ‘Ice Lake’ Datacenter CPUs Make Their Debut, Provide Up to 40 Cores Each
feed.summary: Intel has officially launched its 10nm 'Ice Lake' datacenter CPU lineup, featuring third-generation Xeon Scalable processors with up to 40 cores. These new processors offer significant performance improvements for AI, HPC, networking, and cloud workloads, including an average 46% performance boost for datacenter applications and 53% higher average HPC performance compared to the previous generation. Intel's internal benchmarks also indicate that Ice Lake outperforms AMD's third-generation Epyc Milan processors in key HPC, AI, and cloud applications.

feed.title: AMD's Ryzen 9 5900 Blurs The Line Between Performance And Value
feed.summary: AMD's Ryzen 9 5900, an OEM-exclusive 12-core Zen 3 processor with a 65W TDP, has been spotted in benchmarks. Despite its lower power consumption compared to the 105W Ryzen 9 5900X, early UserBenchmark results suggest it performs within 10% of its more powerful counterpart. The article highlights its appeal as a performance-per-watt chip and Speculates on its potential appearance in the gray market, similar to previous OEM-only APUs.

feed.title: AMD Secretly Drops Radeon RX 6800 XT "Midnight Black" Edition GPU
feed.summary: AMD released a limited edition Radeon RX 6800 XT "Midnight Black" Edition GPU, exclusively announced to its "AMD Red Team Community." Despite efforts, the graphics card, which shares performance specs with the regular version but features a special black shroud, sold out within minutes due to bots and scalpers, making it difficult for fans to acquire. The $649 USD card is now fetching upwards of $1,700 USD on eBay, highlighting ongoing GPU shortage issues and frustrations among gamers.

feed.title: Nvidia steps up competition with Intel and AMD with first data-center CPU, based on Arm
feed.summary: Nvidia introduced its first data-center CPU, named Grace, based on Arm technology, aiming to intensify its competition with Intel and AMD in the artificial-intelligence market. This move signals Nvidia's commitment to the chip-making sector, leveraging technology from its acquisition target, Arm Holdings PLC, to tap into the growing AI business. The announcement was made during Nvidia’s annual GTC developers conference by CEO Jensen Huang.

feed.title: Nvidia steps up competition with Intel and AMD with first data-center CPU, based on Arm
feed.summary: Nvidia introduced its first data-center CPU, named Grace, which is based on Arm technology, intensifying its competition with Intel and AMD in the artificial intelligence and data-center market. This move comes as Nvidia awaits approval for its $40 billion acquisition of Arm Holdings PLC, showcasing its strategy to leverage licensed technology for future AI business. The new CPU, announced at Nvidia's annual GTC developers conference, aims to challenge the dominance of Intel and AMD in the server CPU space.

feed.title: Nvidia steps up competition with Intel and AMD with first data-center CPU, based on Arm
feed.summary: Nvidia introduced its first data-center CPU, named Grace, which is based on Arm technology, intensifying its competition with Intel and AMD in the artificial-intelligence market. This move comes as Nvidia awaits approval for its $40 billion acquisition of Arm Holdings PLC, demonstrating its strategy for future AI business. The new CPU bundle for servers enters a market traditionally dominated by Intel and AMD.

feed.title: Nvidia to directly challenge Intel with Arm-based 'Grace' server chip
feed.summary: Nvidia Corp announced its plan to develop an Arm-based server processor chip called "Grace," directly challenging Intel in the data center market. This move intensifies competition with Intel and adds complexity to the antitrust review of Nvidia's $40 billion acquisition of Arm. The new server chip, expected in 2023, is designed for artificial intelligence algorithms, further solidifying Nvidia's focus on AI computing.

feed.title: NVIDIA Announces CPU for Giant AI and High Performance Computing Workloads
feed.summary: NVIDIA has announced its first data center CPU, Grace, an Arm-based processor designed to deliver 10x the performance of today’s fastest servers for complex AI and high-performance computing workloads. This specialized processor targets advanced applications with enormous datasets and massive memory requirements, making NVIDIA a "three-chip company" alongside its GPUs and DPUs. The Swiss National Supercomputing Centre and Los Alamos National Laboratory are among the first to plan Grace-powered supercomputers, expected online in 2023.

feed.title: Impact Shares NAACP Minority Empowerment ETF (NACP) Stock Price, Quote, News & History
feed.summary: This article provides detailed financial information, news, and historical data for the Impact Shares NAACP Minority Empowerment ETF (NACP). It includes key statistics like AUM, dividend, volume, and P/E ratio, along with recent news and a FAQ section covering how to buy, competitors, and dividend information. The ETF focuses on investing in large and mid-capitalization companies that empower minorities, aiming for similar risk and return characteristics to the Morningstar US Large-Mid Cap Index.

feed.title: Nvidia Reveals Arm-Based Data Center CPU To Take On Intel, AMD
feed.summary: Nvidia has unveiled Grace, an Arm-based data center CPU designed to compete with Intel and AMD in AI and high-performance computing. Grace, expected in early 2023, promises 10 times faster AI performance than AMD's EPYC CPUs and is a key component of Nvidia's expanded data center strategy, which now includes GPUs, DPUs, and CPUs. This move comes as Nvidia progresses with its acquisition of Arm and aims to provide an optimal architecture for trillion-parameter AI models.

feed.title: NVIDIA CEO Introduces Software, Silicon, Supercomputers ‘for the Da Vincis of Our Time’
feed.summary: NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang unveiled a comprehensive vision for the future at GTC, highlighting advancements in AI, automotive, robotics, and virtual worlds. He introduced new hardware like the Grace data center CPU, BlueField-3 DPU, and NVIDIA Atlan for automotive, alongside significant software developments such as NVIDIA AI, Omniverse, and various AI acceleration libraries. Huang emphasized NVIDIA's evolution into a computing platform company offering solutions across GPUs, CPUs, and DPUs, and a robust software ecosystem to empower researchers and enterprises.

feed.title: FPGAでAIに全振りしたAchronix AIプロセッサーの昨今 (1/3)
feed.summary: 本記事では、Achronix SemiconductorのFPGA、特にAIプロセッサーへの応用について解説しています。AchronixはFPGA専業メーカーとして、ニッチながらも「超高速FPGA」やeFPGA IPの提供を通じて市場での存在感を確立してきました。同社の最新製品Speedster7tのサンプル出荷が開始され、AI分野への特化が注目されています。

feed.title: Latest AMD Radeon Software Release Expands Remote Gaming Functionality and Enables New Features and Customization Capabilities
feed.summary: AMD has released Radeon Software Adrenalin 21.4.1, enhancing remote gaming with AMD Link for Windows PCs and adding new features for gamers. The update includes customizable installation options, improved recording and streaming tools, and expanded performance metrics. It also introduces AMD Link Game for shared local multiplayer experiences and incorporates stability features like AMD Crash Defender.

feed.title: GlobalFoundries moving headquarters to Malta
feed.summary: GlobalFoundries announced it is moving its corporate headquarters from Silicon Valley to its Fab 8 manufacturing facility in Malta, New York, effective immediately. CEO Tom Caulfield cited his New York roots and expressed pride in making upstate New York the new headquarters. The move is not expected to cause a major influx of jobs from California but aligns with efforts to boost U.S. chip manufacturing, with potential for a second fabrication facility in Malta pending federal funding and infrastructure improvements.

feed.title: Leaked tests show Nvidia's CMP 40HX mining card performing close to an RTX 3060
feed.summary: Nvidia's CMP 40HX mining card is reportedly performing close to an RTX 3060 in leaked tests. Asus was able to optimize the card's hash rate for Ethereum mining to over 43 MH/s, a significant increase from the advertised 36 MH/s. This improved performance, combined with a projected retail price of around $699, might encourage miners to purchase the CMP 40HX instead of the consumer-geared RTX 3060, leaving more RTX 3060 cards available for gamers.

feed.title: Arm details new data center chips, says Oracle, Alibaba will use them
feed.summary: Arm Ltd revealed new details about its next-generation data center technology, with its "N2" Neoverse computing cores expected to be about 40% faster and "V1" cores 50% faster than previous methods for AI tasks. Oracle Corp and Alibaba Group Holding are set to use these new Arm-based chips, signaling a growing push into the data center processor market long dominated by Intel and AMD. This move is seen as crucial for Nvidia Corp's $40 billion acquisition of Arm, which is currently under national security review.

feed.title: Intel vs Advanced Micro Devices (AMD)
feed.summary: This article analyzes the long-standing rivalry between Intel and AMD, comparing their historical market dominance, recent performance, financial health, and future outlook. It highlights AMD's recent surge due to TSMC's advanced chip manufacturing, challenging Intel's traditional leadership. The author concludes by positioning AMD as a growth play and Intel as a value play, considering potential comebacks and ongoing industry challenges like chip shortages.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) 4th Earnings: What to Expect
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is set to report its first-quarter fiscal 2021 earnings, with expectations rising due to strong performance from rival Intel and increasing chip demand. Analysts predict earnings of 44 cents per share on $3.21 billion in revenue for the quarter, largely driven by gains in datacenter revenue and PC demand, where AMD has been taking market share from Intel. The company's gross margin growth and the recent Xilinx acquisition are also expected to contribute to its continued momentum.

feed.title: Arm Puts Some Muscle Into Future Neoverse Server CPU Designs
feed.summary: Arm is unveiling details about its future Neoverse V1 ("Zeus") and N2 ("Perseus") server CPU designs, aimed at the datacenter and edge. The V1 core is designed for high-performance computing and enterprise workloads, offering significantly better single-threaded integer performance than its predecessor, while the N2 core optimizes for performance per dollar and watt, targeting mainstream infrastructure servers. These new designs, based on the upcoming Armv9-A architecture, highlight Arm's continued push into the server market with tailored solutions.

feed.title: Can Advanced Micro Devices stock build on momentum off huge sales growth?
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock has seen a marginal dip from recent highs, but analysts remain bullish due to significant sales growth, especially in data center products and gaming consoles. CEO Lisa Su's confident outlook on future revenue growth and a strong pipeline suggest continued positive momentum for the company, despite a recent sell-off and a creeping P/E ratio.

feed.title: 'Mad Money' Lightning Round: Plug Power, Coinbase, Pool Corp.
feed.summary: Jim Cramer offers his quick takes on several stocks during the "Mad Money Lightning Round." He shows bullish sentiment for Plug Power and Coinbase, recommends Pool Corp. over Leslie's, and advises against Kellogg in favor of Mondelez International.

feed.title: Apple Finally Feels the Effects of the Global Chip Shortage
feed.summary: Apple Inc. reported strong revenue and record sales in its latest quarter, but warned that the global chip shortage is beginning to impact its business. Executives stated that supply constraints would specifically affect iPad and Mac sales, potentially reducing third-quarter revenue by $3 billion to $4 billion. This marks the first significant impact of the chip shortage on Apple's previously resilient production.

feed.title: UMC to invest $3.6 billion in fab expansion
feed.summary: United Microelectronics Corporation (UMC) announced a $3.6 billion investment to expand its 300mm Fab 12A Phase 6 at Taiwan’s Tainan Science Park, with production starting in Q2 2023. This expansion is part of a broader trend in the semiconductor industry to address a global chip shortage and meet future demand, with other major players like TSMC and Intel also planning significant investments. The increasing need for semiconductors across various industries, from automotive to industrial automation, is fueling this fab construction boom and driving regional efforts to attract chip manufacturing.

feed.title: UMC to up production of 28nm chips amid global shortage
feed.summary: United Microelectronics Corporation (UMC) is increasing its production of 28-nanometer chips by adding capacity for 20,000 wafers per month at an existing Tainan-based fabrication plant. This expansion, costing $2.3 billion, is supported by a unique deal where major customers pay upfront deposits and guarantee orders at fixed prices to address the global semiconductor shortage, particularly in mature nodes. The company and other firms like TSMC, AMD, and Texas Instruments are responding to high demand and strained supply, exacerbated by recent disruptions from events like Texas' Storm Uri and a fire at Renesas.

feed.title: Intel to Spend USD $3.5 Billion to Upgrade New Mexico Factory; More Exciting Chips Soon?
feed.summary: Intel plans to invest $3.5 billion to upgrade its New Mexico factory, aiming to boost domestic chip manufacturing and address the global semiconductor shortage. This move is also a strategic response to competitors like AMD, enabling potentially lower chip costs and increased availability of upcoming products such as the 12th Gen Alder Lake processors and a powerful gaming-grade Xe-HPG graphics card. Intel's investment signals a push towards innovation and expanded in-house production, including custom chips for third-party clients.

feed.title: Intel’s $3.5B New Mexico Campus Upgrade To Boost Next-Gen Chips
feed.summary: Intel is investing $3.5 billion to upgrade its New Mexico manufacturing site, enabling it to produce next-generation processors with Foveros 3D packaging technology. This investment is part of Intel's new IDM 2.0 strategy, which aims to enhance performance, create jobs, and regain leadership in the chip market. The Foveros technology will allow Intel to stack chip tiles vertically, offering greater flexibility and performance in a smaller footprint.

feed.title: Intel’s new CEO Pat Gelsinger says chipmaker will build more Oregon factories
feed.summary: Intel's new CEO Pat Gelsinger announced plans to continue expanding the company's manufacturing footprint in Oregon, intending to add more factories within the next few years. This commitment comes as Intel wraps up a $3 billion expansion in Hillsboro and despite earlier considerations of outsourcing manufacturing. Gelsinger emphasized Oregon's role as the hub for Intel's semiconductor research and is campaigning for government incentives to support the chip industry in the U.S. and Europe, aiming to reduce reliance on Asian manufacturers.

feed.title: Microsoft pulls Windows 10 AMD driver causing PCs not to boot
feed.summary: Microsoft has withdrawn an AMD driver from Windows Update after reports that it caused Windows 10 PCs to fail to boot, displaying an "INACCESSIBLE BOOT DEVICE" error. The problematic driver, 'Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. - SCSIAdapter - 9.3.0.221', was rolled out to incompatible hardware after an initial testing phase, affecting primarily owners of Gigabyte X570 motherboards. Many users were able to fix the issue through Automatic Startup Repair, while others required more complex solutions.

feed.title: AMD SCSIAdapter 9.3.0.221 update is crashing Windows 10 PCs
feed.summary: A recently released AMD SCSIAdapter 9.3.0.221 driver update is causing Windows 10 PCs to crash with a "INACCESSIBLE_BOOT_DEVICE" Blue Screen of Death error. Microsoft has reportedly pulled the problematic driver from Windows Update, though some users on production builds may have already received it. Users are advised to block the update or rollback the driver if already installed.

feed.title: Samsung to jump into laptop processor market with Exynos chip in H2
feed.summary: Samsung Electronics is set to enter the laptop processor market in the second half of this year with a premium Exynos chip, targeting the rapidly growing global laptop market. The new Exynos chip will feature a GPU developed with AMD, focusing on improved computing power and battery efficiency with 5-nanometer processing technology. Samsung's move follows other tech giants like Apple and Qualcomm, who have also developed laptop-grade chipsets, as the company seeks to compete with market leader Intel.

feed.title: Samsung to jump into laptop processor market with Exynos chip in H2
feed.summary: Samsung Electronics plans to release a laptop version of its Exynos mobile application processor in the second half of this year, entering the growing laptop market. This premium Exynos chip, developed with AMD's GPU technology, aims to offer improved computing power and battery efficiency using 5-nanometer technology. Samsung will join other tech companies like Apple and Qualcomm in developing laptop-specific chipsets, capitalizing on the rapid growth in global laptop sales.

feed.title: STMicroelectronics Tackles the EV Industry with GaN
feed.summary: STMicroelectronics is focusing on gallium nitride (GaN) technology to enhance efficiency in electric vehicle applications, recognizing its superiority over silicon in high-power scenarios. The company has released a new family of GaN devices called STi2GaN, specifically designed for the automotive industry, which integrates power stages, drivers, and protection within GaN and SiP solutions. This move aligns with STMicroelectronics' earlier commitment to invest in wide-bandgap semiconductors for carbon neutrality goals.

feed.title: Intel Launches New 11th Gen Core for Mobile
feed.summary: Intel Corporation (INTC) announced the global launch of its 11th Generation Intel Core H-series mobile processors, code-named "Tiger Lake-H," on May 11, 2021. The flagship Intel Core i9-11980HK, based on 10nm SuperFin process technology, delivers speeds up to 5.0GHz, offering industry-leading mobile performance for gaming, content creation, and business professionals with up to eight cores and 16 threads, and PCIe Gen 4 support. The release also includes new Intel vPro H-series and Intel Xeon W-11000 series mobile processors, powering over 80 enthusiast laptop designs this year.

feed.title: Mythic Raises $70 Million in Series C Funding Led by BlackRock and Hewlett Packard Enterprise
feed.summary: Mythic, a pioneering analog AI processor company, announced it has raised $70 million in Series C funding. This round was led by BlackRock and Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE), bringing Mythic's total funding to $165.2 million. The new capital will be used to accelerate mass production of its AI inference solutions, expand customer support, and develop its next-generation hardware and software.

feed.title: Samsung and AMD will reportedly take on Apple’s M1 SoC later this year
feed.summary: Samsung is reportedly accelerating its Exynos SoC development with an AMD-powered GPU, aiming to compete with Apple's M1 chip in laptops by the second half of this year. While this partnership could boost Samsung's GPU capabilities for laptops, current Exynos smartphone chips are not expected to be dramatically changed, and Samsung still faces challenges in CPU design and the Windows-on-ARM ecosystem compared to Apple's integrated approach. The article also touches on Qualcomm's Nuvia acquisition and Google's "Whitechapel" ambitions, highlighting the broader race to develop competitive ARM-based processors.

feed.title: AWS, HPE, Other IT Vendors Back $50B Call For U.S. Chip Production
feed.summary: A coalition including major IT vendors like AWS, HPE, Apple, and Microsoft is advocating for $50 billion in U.S. government funding for domestic semiconductor manufacturing and research. This initiative, part of the CHIPS for America Act, aims to address the decline in U.S. chip production share and bolster supply chain resiliency. While supporting the funding, the group advised against government intervention in the current chip shortage and urged careful allocation of funds to benefit the entire industry rather than individual companies.

feed.title: 10 Hot Data Center Technologies And Trends To Watch In 2021
feed.summary: This article outlines ten significant data center technologies and trends for 2021, driven by the shift to remote work and a digital economy. Key themes include the rise of "as-a-service" models, massive investments by public cloud providers, increasing private equity in data centers, and heightened CPU innovation from Intel, AMD, and Nvidia. Additionally, the article highlights the growing importance of automation, sustainability, and edge computing in the data center industry.

feed.title: Corsair's DDR5 primer has me dreaming of running 1TB of RAM
feed.summary: Corsair has released a DDR5 primer, detailing the advancements of the next-generation RAM standard. DDR5 promises increased bandwidth and denser capacities, with mainstream motherboards potentially supporting up to 1TB of memory. While higher CAS latencies exist, Corsair explains that architectural changes will result in similar or better overall latency compared to DDR4.

feed.title: S Korea unveils US$450bn chipmaking master plan
feed.summary: South Korea has announced a monumental US$450 billion investment plan over the next decade to build the world’s largest chipmaking base, involving key players like Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix. This initiative, dubbed the "K-semiconductor belt," aims to boost semiconductor research and production, enhance the nation's advanced logic chip capabilities, and reduce reliance on external manufacturers, amidst a global race for chip dominance and ongoing supply shortages. The government will support the industry with significant tax breaks, infrastructure improvements, and training programs to compete with global leaders like TSMC.

feed.title: GTA 6 may release in Take-Two's FY2024 (April 2023 - March 2024)
feed.summary: Take-Two Interactive's financial guidance suggests Grand Theft Auto 6 could be released during the company's FY2024, which spans April 2023 to March 2024. The company anticipates record earnings in FY2024, which aligns with a major title like GTA 6. Although not officially confirmed, this timing is inferred from Take-Two's projected growth and plans for over 40 new titles by FY2024, including "immersive core" games.

feed.title: ADI Infographics: All you need to know about Analog Devices Q2 2021 earnings results
feed.summary: Analog Devices, Inc. (ADI) reported strong Q2 2021 financial results, with total revenues increasing 26% to $1.66 billion and record revenue in the Industrial and Automotive markets. The company's net income for the quarter was $422 million, or $1.14 per share, significantly up from $267 million, or $0.72 per share, in Q2 2020.

feed.title: The TJX Companies (TJX) Q1 2022 Earnings: Key financials and quarterly highlights
feed.summary: The TJX Companies reported strong financial results for Q1 2022, with net income reaching $533.9 million, or $0.44 per share, reversing a net loss from the prior year. Net sales significantly increased by 126% to $10.1 billion. CEO Ernie Herrman expressed optimism about the company's strong positioning post-health crisis and its opportunities for global market share expansion.

feed.title: AMD CEO predicts supply of TSMC-built chips to improve
feed.summary: AMD CEO Lisa Su addressed current chip shortages, stating they are cyclical and not a "disaster," despite the semiconductor firm's increasingly tough path. Under Su's leadership, AMD has seen significant growth, with its market value surging to over US$90 billion, leading to a recent US$4 billion stock-repurchase plan. She emphasized the competitive nature of the market and AMD's ambitions amidst accelerated demand for computing resources due to the Covid-19 pandemic.

feed.title: Which Sectors Saw the Biggest Valuation Boosts?
feed.summary: Morningstar equity analysts increased fair value estimates for cyclical stocks at a faster pace during the first-quarter earnings season, driven by an improved U.S. economic outlook and aggressive vaccination rollout. The basic materials, communication services, and technology sectors saw the most significant fair value estimate increases, primarily due to rising commodity prices, improved digital advertising spending, and semiconductor demand exceeding supply, respectively. Financial services and industrials also experienced notable valuation boosts.

feed.title: AMD’s path only becomes tougher from here: CEO
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) CEO Lisa Su reflects on the company's significant turnaround under her leadership, increasing market value and returning to profitability. Despite these successes, Su emphasizes that the competitive landscape means the challenges ahead will only intensify. She notes that while recent chip shortages highlight industry imbalances, they have also fostered stronger long-term commitments between suppliers and customers.

feed.title: Lisa Su Secures 731 Million Votes In AMD Election – Talks About Tight Supply Chain
feed.summary: Dr. Lisa Su, CEO of Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), received an overwhelming 732 million votes in favor of her re-election to the company's board of directors during their annual shareholder meeting. This strong support comes as AMD announced a $4 billion share buyback program, the first since 2001, indicating management confidence in its financial position. Additionally, Dr. Su addressed the tight semiconductor supply chain, expressing optimism about AMD's ability to meet its 50% annual revenue growth target for 2021 through strong partnerships.

feed.title: Putting the Chip Shortage into the Context of Long-Term Trends
feed.summary: The article analyzes the current global semiconductor shortage, attributing it to supply chain rigidities, high barriers to entry, and geopolitical tensions exacerbated by COVID-19. It highlights the long-term trends driving increased semiconductor demand, such as 5G, AI, and electric vehicles, and discusses how governments and companies are responding to secure future supply chains and potentially re-shore manufacturing.

feed.title: Samsung, TSMC up ante in US foundry competition
feed.summary: The world's two largest foundry chipmakers, TSMC and Samsung Electronics, are engaged in fierce competition to expand their presence in the US, driven by significant investment plans. Samsung announced a $17 billion investment for a US factory, following TSMC's $12 billion Arizona plant. This rivalry, coupled with Intel's entry into the foundry business, intensifies the competition in the advanced chip manufacturing market, with both companies risking substantial losses if they fail to secure enough clients for their costly new facilities.

feed.title: Oracle debuts its first Arm-based cloud instances
feed.summary: Oracle has launched its first Arm-based cloud instances, OCI Ampere A1 Compute, offering customers more flexibility and high performance at lower costs for general-purpose and cloud-native workloads. This move signifies Oracle's increased investment in the Arm ecosystem, providing a compelling alternative to x86-based instances and competing with rival cloud providers by offering attractive pricing and scaling options. The company is also expanding developer resources and tools to encourage Arm-based application development on its cloud platform, including free tier offerings and a new development stack.

feed.title: Announcing NGINX Plus support for the Arm Compute shapes on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure
feed.summary: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) now officially supports NGINX Plus on its Arm-based Ampere A1 Compute instances (VM and bare metal shapes). This integration allows developers to leverage NGINX Plus for cloud-native functionalities, enhanced performance, and robust security, taking advantage of the cost-effectiveness and high performance of OCI's Arm architecture, which demonstrably outperforms Xeon and EPYC instances in performance per dollar. The partnership aims to provide an open cloud environment, avoiding vendor lock-in and supporting diverse developer ecosystems.

feed.title: Intel vs. AMD — should you buy either stock now?
feed.summary: This article contrasts Intel and AMD, two dominant players in the processor market, from an investment perspective. While AMD has shown significant outperformance under CEO Lisa Su, Intel remains the largest provider of core processors for PCs and servers, boasting a substantial R&D budget and strong cash flow. The piece questions which stock is a better buy for investors, acknowledging Intel's new CEO Pat Gelsinger's efforts to diversify and fend off AMD's market share gains.

feed.title: Intel vs. AMD — should you buy either stock now?
feed.summary: This article analyzes the long-standing rivalry between Intel and AMD in the core processor market. While AMD has shown significant market-share growth and superior stock performance, Intel remains a dominant force with a large R&D budget and strong cash flow. The piece explores the investment merits of each company for those considering buying either stock.

feed.title: Marvell Introduces Bravera SSD Controllers to Enable the Highest Performing Data Center Flash Storage Solutions
feed.summary: Marvell has unveiled its new Bravera SC5 SSD controller family, which are the industry's first PCIe 5.0 SSD controllers. These controllers are designed to offer unprecedented performance, efficiency, and security for cloud data center workloads, boasting double the performance of PCIe 4.0 SSDs. They also include features like FIPS-compliant root of trust, AES 256-bit encryption, and a hardware-based Elastic SLA Enforcer to ensure quality of service and optimized resource utilization for hyperscale cloud providers.

feed.title: Amy Chang, Calvin McDonald Elected to The Walt Disney Company Board of Directors
feed.summary: The Walt Disney Company has announced the election of technology executive Amy Chang and lululemon CEO Calvin McDonald to its Board of Directors, effective immediately. Both new members bring extensive experience in technology, consumer experience, and scaling major brands. Their appointments are expected to enhance Disney's focus on innovation and connecting with consumers.

feed.title: AMD's Zen 5 CPUs, APUs Will Likely Tap TSMC's 3nm Process Node
feed.summary: Rumors suggest that AMD's upcoming Zen 5 processors, expected around 2023, will utilize TSMC's 3nm manufacturing process node. These chips are anticipated to fall under the Ryzen 8000 branding, with codenames like Granite Ridge for mainstream CPUs and Strix Point for APUs. Strix Point APUs might feature a hybrid architecture combining Zen 5 and Zen 4 cores, potentially using a multi-chip module design.

feed.title: Dell, HP say chip shortages will hit PC supplies this year
feed.summary: Dell Technologies Inc and HP Inc both reported quarterly revenues that surpassed Wall Street estimates due to continued high demand for personal computers. However, shares for both companies dropped after they warned that ongoing chip shortages would impact their ability to meet laptop demand this year. The companies remain optimistic about the overall PC market, expecting sustained demand for remote work and education, despite the supply chain challenges.

feed.title: NVIDIA AI Enterprise Software Drives New Wave of Certified Systems from World’s Leading Manufacturers
feed.summary: NVIDIA announced a significant expansion of its NVIDIA-Certified Systems program, featuring dozens of new servers from leading manufacturers that are certified to run NVIDIA AI Enterprise software. These systems, including high-volume x86 servers, are designed to bring the power of AI to various industries by supporting demanding workloads in traditional data centers and hybrid clouds. The expansion also includes new servers equipped with BlueField-2 DPUs for enhanced security and upcoming Arm CPU servers for high-performance AI computing.

feed.title: It’s Time for Ti: NVIDIA Introduces Gaming Flagship — the GeForce RTX 3080 Ti
feed.summary: NVIDIA has announced two new additions to its GeForce RTX family: the GeForce RTX 3080 Ti and GeForce RTX 3070 Ti. The RTX 3080 Ti, launching June 3rd at $1,199, is touted as the new flagship gaming GPU, offering 1.5x faster performance than previous generations and catering to demanding modern games and creative applications. The RTX 3070 Ti, available next week starting at $599, also delivers significant performance improvements over its predecessors.

feed.title: NVIDIA Partners Unveil New Servers Featuring NVIDIA BlueField DPUs
feed.summary: Several top server manufacturers, including ASUS, Dell Technologies, GIGABYTE, QCT, and Supermicro, announced new systems at COMPUTEX 2021 featuring NVIDIA BlueField-2 Data Processing Units (DPUs). These DPUs enhance server performance, security, and manageability by offloading networking, storage, and cybersecurity tasks from the CPU, making more resources available for applications. The servers are designed for various enterprise applications and can be easily programmed using the NVIDIA DOCA SDK, with DPU-accelerated servers expected to be available later this year.

feed.title: Chip giants are making more money than ever as the semiconductor shortage rages
feed.summary: The world's 10 largest chip manufacturing companies achieved record revenues in the first quarter of 2021, totaling $22.75 billion, amidst a global semiconductor shortage. Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Corp. (TSMC) was a major contributor, accounting for 57% of global chip foundry revenues. The shortage, expected to last until 2023, is driven by high demand across various industries and production disruptions, leading to increased wafer prices and projected continued revenue growth for foundries.

feed.title: AMD shows how FidelityFX Super Resolution beats out NVIDIA's DLSS
feed.summary: AMD announced FidelityFX Super Resolution (FSR) at Computex 2021, an image upscaling technology designed to boost frame rates and resolution. FSR directly competes with NVIDIA's DLSS but offers broader compatibility, supporting a wide range of AMD GPUs and even some NVIDIA GeForce cards. The technology, which provides up to a 2.5 times increase in performance mode, is expected to launch on June 22, 2021.

feed.title: TSMC Throws The Ball In Intel’s Court By Showcasing Customized Chipmaking Technologies
feed.summary: TSMC announced new customized semiconductor nodes, N5A for automotive and N6RF for communications, at its online technology symposium. This move aims to solidify TSMC's position as a contract chip manufacturer by offering specialized processes, directly challenging Intel's plans to enter the contract manufacturing space. TSMC emphasized its commitment to not competing with its customers, differentiating itself from other potential foundries.

feed.title: Chief Executive Leadership Institute Honors Dr. Lisa Su with Legend in Leadership Award
feed.summary: Dr. Lisa Su, CEO of Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), will receive the Legend in Leadership award from the Yale Chief Executive Leadership Institute during the virtual Yale CEO Summit. The award recognizes her transformative leadership at AMD, where she significantly increased the company's market capitalization and improved product performance, positioning AMD as a leader in high-performance computing. This honor also acknowledges her role as the first woman to lead a major chipmaker, inspiring future leaders.

feed.title: Micron Demos DDR5 RDIMM, Expands DDR5 Technology Enablement Program
feed.summary: Micron has showcased its DDR5 registered memory modules (RDIMMs) for servers and announced that its DDR5 Technology Enablement Program (TEP) has grown to include 250 members from over 100 companies. The company is on track for DDR5 memory volume production later in the year, coinciding with the expected availability of new platforms like Intel's Alder Lake for client PCs and Sapphire Rapids for servers, which will support DDR5. Micron's TEP provides crucial technical information and samples to expedite DDR5 adoption across the industry.

feed.title: Super Micro Computer To Double Their Manufacturing Capacity To Meet Worldwide Server And Storage Demands
feed.summary: Super Micro Computer, Inc. is doubling its manufacturing capacity at its US and Taiwan campuses to meet the growing global demand for servers and storage driven by Cloud, AI, and 5G/Edge technologies. This expansion aims to increase production to over two million servers per year by mid-2021 and enable the company to ship fully tested racks directly to customers. Supermicro will offer a range of application-optimized systems, including those supporting the latest Intel and AMD processors, NVIDIA GPUs, and liquid cooling options, to accommodate various workloads.

feed.title: Physics Based Modelling for Power Electronic System Design and Validation Using Ansys
feed.summary: This article discusses the increasing challenges in power electronic system design and validation, particularly in the automotive sector, driven by miniaturization and demand for extended component life. It highlights how physics-based modeling and simulation, using tools like Ansys, can address these complexities by enabling early assessment of thermal, structural, and electromagnetic reliability, thus saving costs and accelerating time to market. The piece emphasizes the importance of simulation for component characterization, reliability studies, and system integration in electric vehicles before physical prototyping.

feed.title: HP, Dell adjust supply chains as shortages linger
feed.summary: HP and Dell executives revealed that the industry is grappling with a widespread component shortage, particularly semiconductors, which is hiking manufacturing costs and causing supply constraints expected to last through 2021. Both companies are responding by adjusting their supply chains, with HP increasing inventories and seeking longer-term supplier contracts, and Dell prioritizing more valuable customers for available components. The article also highlights that a broader issue of multiple simultaneous shortages stems from high consumer demand, plant fires, and winter storms impacting production facilities, leading to a significant imbalance between demand and supply.

feed.title: AMD extends FidelityFX Super Resolution to RX 470 and RX 480
feed.summary: AMD is extending its FidelityFX Super Resolution (FSR) technology to older graphics cards, specifically the Radeon RX 470 and RX 480 GPUs, dating back to 2016. This digital upscaling technology is designed to improve game performance and visuals without requiring significant processing power, and it will also work with a wide range of other AMD and some NVIDIA GeForce cards. FSR is expected to launch on June 22, 2021, offering performance boosts for both high-end and older hardware.

feed.title: Samsung Elec, SK hynix’s memory supremacy challenged by Micron tech advance
feed.summary: Micron Technology Inc. is challenging the memory supremacy of Samsung Electronics and SK hynix by advancing its 1α node-based LPDDR4x DRAM and 176-layer 3D NAND flash mass production. Micron's strategic moves include expanding its A3 DRAM facility and hiring experienced engineers, while Korean competitors are perceived to be lagging due to delayed commercialization and a focus on non-memory chips. This indicates a potential shift in the global memory chip market landscape.

feed.title: Marvell Extends PAM4 DSP Cloud Data Center Leadership with Industry's First Integrated Solution
feed.summary: Marvell has announced its Atlas 50Gbps PAM4 DSP chipset solution, which integrates transimpedance amplifiers and laser drivers into a single mainstream CMOS technology solution. This advancement significantly reduces power consumption by 25% and simplifies the supply chain for high-performance cloud data centers, computing, and AI applications. The Atlas DSP platform is designed for high-volume markets, offering cost-effective, highly integrated, and reliable technology essential for massive data center growth.

feed.title: Oracle shows off more ‘Always Free’ cloud resources for developers
feed.summary: Oracle has significantly expanded its "Always Free" cloud services for developers, adding 13 new offerings to its Oracle Cloud Infrastructure platform, bringing the total to 20. These free resources aim to attract developers to OCI by allowing them to experiment with services, including the new Arm-based Ampere A1 Compute, Oracle JSON Database, NoSQL Database Cloud Service, and low-code application development tools. This expansion positions Oracle more competitively against rivals like AWS and Google Cloud by providing substantial free tiers for building and testing end-to-end applications.

feed.title: Dell announces new AMD-based Integrated Systems for Azure Stack HCI
feed.summary: Dell Technologies Inc. has launched new AMD-based configurations for its Dell EMC Integrated Systems for Microsoft Azure Stack HCI, designed to handle more complex workloads and offer enhanced performance. These systems feature AMD EPYC 2nd generation CPUs and incorporate advanced security features from both AMD and Dell. Additionally, software updates aim to simplify provisioning and delivery tasks for IT engineers by pre-installing the Azure Stack HCI operating system and integrating OpenManage software with Microsoft Windows Admin Center.

feed.title: AMD's new Radeon PRO W6000 Series graphics bring professional power
feed.summary: AMD has launched its new Radeon PRO W6000 series workstation graphics cards, built on the RDNA 2 architecture, offering significant performance improvements over previous generations. The top-tier Radeon PRO W6800 is available immediately, while the Radeon PRO W6600 and W6600M models are set to release later in the year, targeting demanding professional workloads. These GPUs feature advancements like AMD Infinity Cache and Smart Access Memory, delivering up to 79% faster performance.

feed.title: New AMD Radeon PRO W6000 Series Workstation Graphics with AMD RDNA 2 Architecture and Massive 32GB of Memory to Power Demanding Architectural, Design and Media Workloads
feed.summary: AMD announced its new Radeon PRO W6000 series workstation graphics cards, leveraging the AMD RDNA 2 architecture. These cards, including the Radeon PRO W6800 and W6600, are designed to significantly boost performance for demanding professional workloads in architecture, design, and media, offering features like hardware-accelerated raytracing and Smart Access Memory. The Radeon PRO W6800, featuring 32GB of GDDR6 memory, is highlighted as the fastest RDNA workstation graphics card, delivering up to 79% faster performance than its predecessor.

feed.title: Opinion: AMD’s stock has been left behind, but not for long — here’s why
feed.summary: The article argues that Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock is a good buy despite its significant past gains, as it hasn't fully rebounded from a recent dip unlike other semiconductor stocks like Nvidia. The author attributes this potential for future growth to AMD's new cutting-edge products and a key acquisition.

feed.title: AMD EPYC™ Processors Enable Next Generation of Hewlett Packard Enterprise Storage
feed.summary: AMD EPYC™ processors are now powering the new Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) Alletra 6000, a cloud-native data infrastructure storage solution. This integration leverages the high performance and PCIe® 4 capabilities of AMD EPYC 7002 series processors, offering up to three times faster performance than previous HPE Nimble Storage All Flash Arrays. The partnership aims to deliver fast, consistent performance with leading data efficiency for business-critical applications and supports HPE's Unified DataOps vision through HPE GreenLake.

feed.title: AMD: Enterprise Server Sales Will Catch Up With Overall EPYC Growth
feed.summary: AMD expects its EPYC server processor sales to enterprise customers to soon match the growth rate of its overall server CPU business, which doubled last year. Dan McNamara, head of AMD's EPYC business, highlighted that the company is gaining trust with CIOs and expanding its footprint with hyperscalers for internal workloads. This growth is driven by modernizing data centers, AMD's focus on solutions, and platform support from major OEMs, enabling AMD to better compete with Intel.

feed.title: Report: Intel has offered $2B+ to acquire chip startup SiFive
feed.summary: Intel Corp. has reportedly offered over $2 billion to acquire SiFive Inc., a chip startup specializing in processor technology based on the open-source RISC-V instruction set architecture. This potential acquisition would be one of Intel's largest recent deals, following its 2019 purchase of Habana Labs. The move could position Intel to address future demand for open-source chip designs, especially given concerns surrounding Nvidia's acquisition of Arm.

feed.title: Invest in ETFs
feed.summary: VanEck offers a variety of ETFs covering timeless themes like global equity, gold miners, and real estate, as well as smart themes such as semiconductors, video gaming, esports, and hydrogen. Each ETF is designed to support different investment goals but comes with specific risks that investors should carefully assess against their individual objectives. The article emphasizes conducting due diligence by referring to relevant documentation like the prospectus and Key Information Document (KID) before investing.

feed.title: Rumor: Intel May Buy RISC-V CPU Designer SiFive to Fend off ARM
feed.summary: A rumor suggests Intel is considering purchasing RISC-V CPU design company SiFive for $2.1 billion. This acquisition could provide Intel with a significant stake in the expanding RISC-V market, complementing its foundry efforts and potentially positioning RISC-V alongside x86 in specific market segments. The move is also seen as a strategic response to Nvidia's potential acquisition of ARM, allowing Intel to hedge its bets in the evolving CPU landscape, though SiFive's open-source nature means other companies could still develop RISC-V CPUs.

feed.title: Invesco PHLX Semiconductor ETF (SOXQ) Stock Price, Quote, News & History
feed.summary: This page provides a comprehensive overview of the Invesco PHLX Semiconductor ETF (SOXQ), including its current stock price, key statistics, news, and historical data. It details the ETF's sector, assets under management, dividend information, and top holdings, along with recent news articles about related semiconductor companies like Intel and Advanced Micro Devices. The page also includes an FAQ section answering common investor questions about buying SOXQ, its competitors, and future outlook.

feed.title: Start-up: From engineer to IoT entrepreneur
feed.summary: Teng Kong Leong transitioned from an engineer at multinational companies to an IoT entrepreneur, founding Wondernica Research PLT in 2014 and later Wondernica Technologies Sdn Bhd in 2018. The company specializes in end-to-end IoT solutions for smart cities, focusing on smart agriculture and environmental monitoring, and is expanding into cold chain logistics. Despite slow IoT adoption in Malaysia, Wondernica is gaining traction internationally and through pilot projects, aiming to provide affordable solutions by designing both hardware and software.

feed.title: Dell Beats HPE In Global Server Market Share Fight
feed.summary: Dell Technologies emerged as the global server market share leader in Q1 2021, surpassing Hewlett Packard Enterprise as the overall market grew 12 percent year over year to $20.9 billion. Dell captured 17 percent of the market with $3.56 billion in server revenue and led in server unit shipments, while HPE/H3C ranked second. Other key players like Inspur, Lenovo, and IBM also showed significant performance, with ODM direct sales continuing to represent the largest segment.

feed.title: Taiwan Semiconductor Is the World’s Most Important Chip Maker. How to Play the Stock.
feed.summary: The article highlights Taiwan Semiconductor (TSMC) as the world's most critical chipmaker, responsible for approximately 60% of outsourced chip manufacturing and 90% of the industry's profits. It notes that the ongoing semiconductor shortage has emphasized the importance of chips across various sectors, from simple components to high-end processors. The article then addresses how investors can approach this significant stock.

feed.title: Checks in the Mail from Coach, AMD, Verizon, San Diego Airport Car Rental Settlements
feed.summary: Top Class Actions reports that consumers are receiving settlement checks from several class action lawsuits. These include second payments for the AMD CPUs class action, payouts for Coach Outlet discount pricing, San Diego Airport car rental fees, and the Verizon Wireless Family SharePlan settlement. The article details the amounts received and the deadlines for filing claims in these respective cases.

feed.title: Rambus Advances New Era of Data Center Architecture with CXL™ Memory Interconnect Initiative
feed.summary: Rambus Inc. announced the CXL Memory Interconnect Initiative to develop semiconductor solutions for advanced data center architectures, focusing on memory expansion and pooling. This initiative aims to maximize performance, improve efficiency, and reduce system costs in data centers by leveraging Rambus' expertise in high-speed interfaces, embedded security, and server memory buffers. The acquisitions of PLDA and AnalogX are noted as key accelerants for Rambus' CXL roadmap and market leadership in this domain.

feed.title: Rambus in two deals for datacentre interface
feed.summary: Rambus has acquired AnalogX and PLDA to strengthen its position in data center interface IP, particularly for the Compute Express Link (CXL) standard. These acquisitions will accelerate Rambus' roadmap for PCIe 6.0 and CXL 3.0 solutions and bolster its CXL Memory Interconnect Initiative, aiming to develop next-generation memory interfaces for high-speed data center connectivity. The deals are expected to enhance Rambus' offerings for AI/ML and 5G applications and contribute to a paradigm shift in data center architectures.

feed.title: Kopin Pancake Optics to ‘Reshape’ MR Headsets
feed.summary: Kopin Corp has developed fully-plastic Pancake optics designed to significantly improve virtual, augmented, and mixed reality headsets. These new optics offer magnified image quality with a thinner and lighter form factor, aiming to overcome the bulkiness that has hindered consumer adoption. Kopin has filed multiple patents for this technology, which promises to make MR headsets much lighter and more compact.

feed.title: AWS’ secret weapon is revolutionizing computing
feed.summary: Amazon Web Services (AWS) is pioneering a new system architecture through specialized designs like Nitro and Graviton, which move away from inefficient centralized processing. This strategic shift allows AWS to support diverse workloads from cloud to edge computing, offering significant cost and performance advantages. The author believes this approach will define the future of computing across the industry, with Intel and VMware also developing similar "Nitro-like" solutions.

feed.title: Intel to work with India’s Reliance Jio on 5G network technology
feed.summary: Intel Corp announced a partnership with India's Reliance Jio to co-develop 5G networking technology. This collaboration stems from Intel's venture capital unit's $250 million investment in Jio Platforms last year. The companies will focus on "co-innovations" for Reliance Jio's 5G radio-access network.

feed.title: A big stock award helped a CEO jump to the top of the list of highest paid female execs
feed.summary: AMD CEO Lisa Su has become the highest-paid female executive in the Bay Area, largely due to a substantial $42 million stock award. This achievement has placed her at the top of the list, contrasting with last year's top two female executives who did not appear on this year's ranking. The report highlights the significant impact of stock awards on executive compensation.

feed.title: This is Grand Theft Auto as made by AI
feed.summary: AI researchers have utilized Nvidia's GameGAN neural network to create "GAN Theft Auto," an entirely AI-generated version of Grand Theft Auto V. The playable demo showcases an AI-generated highway scene that, despite being pixelated, demonstrates impressive details like shadows and reflections, all without human-coded graphics. This project offers a glimpse into a future where entire games, or portions thereof, could be concocted by AI.

feed.title: Longtime Intel exec Navin Shenoy leaves as chipmaker restructures data center business
feed.summary: Longtime Intel executive Navin Shenoy is departing the company as CEO Pat Gelsinger implements a major restructuring of its critical data center business, creating two new units. The Data Platform Group is being split into a Datacenter and AI group led by Sandra Rivera, and a Network and Edge Group led by Nick McKeown. Additionally, Intel is forming a Software and Advanced Technology Group and an Accelerated Computing Systems and Graphics Group, led by Greg Lavender and Raja Koduri respectively, to better compete with rivals like Nvidia, Arm Ltd., Qualcomm Corp., and Advanced Micro Devices Inc.

feed.title: Intel Restructuring: Navin Shenoy Out; New Software, GPU Units
feed.summary: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger has announced a major restructuring of the company, leading to the departure of top data center executive Navin Shenoy. The Data Platform Group is being split into two new business units, and two new divisions, the Software and Advanced Technology Group and the Accelerated Computing Systems and Graphics Group, are being formed. These changes aim to accelerate Intel's pace in regaining technology leadership, with new leaders appointed to head the reorganized groups.

feed.title: GlobalFoundries to open new fab in Singapore
feed.summary: GlobalFoundries is investing $4 billion to build a new semiconductor fabrication plant in Singapore, aiming to expand its manufacturing capacity amidst a global chip shortage. The facility, expected to roll out its first production wafers in Q1 2023, will create 1,000 high-value jobs and strengthen Singapore's position in advanced manufacturing. This expansion is designed to meet surging demand driven by 5G, IoT, and cloud computing.

feed.title: HPE should add bank card swipe to on-prem cloud for growth, says analyst
feed.summary: A disruptive technology analyst suggests that HPE's on-premises cloud solutions, like GreenLake, should incorporate credit card payment options to offer developers greater flexibility and agility, mimicking public cloud advantages. This would enable easy scaling of workloads and position HPE to capitalize on the shift of data and compute to the edge. The article emphasizes the importance of edge computing, detailing the challenges and opportunities for on-prem vendors, and also touches on the future of the semiconductor industry with increased competition.

feed.title: New Intel XPU Innovations Target HPC and AI
feed.summary: Intel is showcasing new innovations at the 2021 International Supercomputing Conference (ISC), extending its leadership in high-performance computing (HPC) and AI. Key announcements include the integration of High Bandwidth Memory (HBM) in the next-gen Intel Xeon Scalable processors ("Sapphire Rapids"), the powered-on Xe-HPC-based Ponte Vecchio GPU, new high-performance networking with Ethernet, and commercial support for DAOS storage. These advancements aim to deliver significant performance boosts for HPC applications and accelerate the industry's move towards exascale computing.
